response:
16469 is not a prime number because it can be factored as 43 × 383.

\boxed{\text{no}}
reasoning_content: We need to determine if 16469 is a prime number. Check if it has any divisors other than 1 and itself. Test small primes: 2: odd, so no. 3: sum of digits = 1+6+4+6+9=26, not divisible by 3. 5: last digit not 0 or 5, so no. 7: 7*2352=16464, remainder 5, so not divisible. 11: alternating sum: 1-6+4-6+9=2, not divisible by 11. 13: 13*1266=16458, remainder 11; 13*1267=16471 too high. 17: 17*968=16456, remainder 13; 17*969=16473. 19: 19*867=16473? Actually 19*867 = 19*800=15200, 19*67=1273, sum=16473, so too high; 19*866=16454, remainder 15. 23: 23*716=16468, remainder 1; 23*717=16491 too high. 29: 29*568=16472? Actually 29*568=29*500=14500, 29*68=1972, total=16472, remainder -3; 29*567=16443, remainder 26. 31: 31*531=16461, remainder 8; 31*532=16492. 37: 37*445=16465, remainder 4; 37*446=16502. 41: 41*401=16441, remainder 28; 41*402=16482. 43: 43*383=16469? Let's calculate: 43*383. 40*383=15320, 3*383=1149, sum=16469 exactly. So 16469 = 43 * 383. Therefore it is not prime. Answer: no.
